context.story_engine: Multiple noble families vie for power across the Seven Kingdoms while ancient threats stir beyond the Wall, forcing characters to navigate deadly political intrigue where honor and survival rarely align.

plotlines.name: Ned: Investigation
plotlines.goal: uncover the truth about Jon Arryn's death and the royal succession
plotlines.obstacle: Lannister power, court intrigue, and political machinations
plotlines.stakes: his life, family safety, and the realm's stability

plotlines.name: Jon: Watch
plotlines.goal: prove himself worthy at the Night's Watch and defend the realm
plotlines.obstacle: prejudice from fellow recruits and ancient threats beyond the Wall
plotlines.stakes: his honor, duty to the realm, and the safety of Westeros

plotlines.name: Daenerys: Transformation
plotlines.goal: find her place and power in a foreign culture
plotlines.obstacle: abuse, cultural barriers, and her brother's control
plotlines.stakes: her survival, identity, and potential claim to the Iron Throne

plotlines.name: Tyrion: Survival
plotlines.goal: survive false accusations and family conflicts
plotlines.obstacle: Catelyn's arrest, Lysa's judgment, and family dynamics

plotlines.name: Catelyn: Protection
plotlines.goal: protect her family from the Lannister threat
plotlines.obstacle: political machinations, war, and divided loyalties
plotlines.stakes: her family's destruction and the North's independence

plotlines.name: Robb: War
plotlines.goal: rescue his father and defend family honor through military action
plotlines.obstacle: Lannister military power and political maneuvering

events.event: Three Night's Watch rangers discover wildlings slaughtered in ritual pattern
White Walker kills two rangers, Will flees south
Ned executes deserter Will for abandoning his post
Stark children find dead direwolf with pups, each claims one
Raven brings news of Jon Arryn's death
King Robert arrives at Winterfell with the Lannisters
Robert visits Lyanna's crypt and asks Ned to be Hand of the King
Robert proposes marriage between Joffrey and Sansa
Viserys arranges Daenerys's marriage to Khal Drogo for his army
Daenerys submits to the marriage arrangement despite her terror
Jorah Mormont presents himself to Daenerys at the wedding
Catelyn receives Lysa's letter accusing Lannisters of murdering Jon Arryn
Ned decides he must go south to investigate after reading the letter
Bran discovers Cersei and Jaime in sexual embrace while climbing
Jaime pushes Bran from the window to protect their secret

episodes.theme: the price of truth in a world built on lies

interactions.description: all three plotlines explore characters forced into new roles by circumstances beyond their control
the Lannister secret connects Robert's succession crisis with Ned's investigation and threatens Catelyn's family
the audience knows Joffrey is illegitimate while Robert plans the royal succession around him

events.event: Maester Luwin tells Catelyn and Robb that Bran will never walk again if he lives
Catelyn refuses to leave Bran's bedside, neglecting Winterfell's affairs
Jaime expresses unease to Cersei about what Bran might remember
Cersei dismisses the risk, confident a child's word means nothing
Jon decides to join the Night's Watch and travels north with Tyrion
Jon gives Arya a small sword she names Needle
Jon says farewell to the unconscious Bran
Ned departs Winterfell for King's Landing with Sansa and Arya
Sansa and Joffrey encounter Arya sparring with Mycah by the Trident
Joffrey threatens Mycah with his sword
Nymeria bites Joffrey's arm in defense of Arya
Arya drives Nymeria away to save the wolf from punishment
Robert holds court on the matter of the direwolf incident
Cersei demands a direwolf be killed for attacking Joffrey
Robert orders Sansa's innocent direwolf Lady executed instead
Ned carries out the execution of Lady himself
The Hound hunts down and kills the butcher's boy Mycah
Daenerys struggles with painful nights with Drogo and grueling days of riding
Jorah rides alongside Daenerys and explains Dothraki customs
Daenerys learns from her handmaiden how to assert herself with Drogo
Daenerys faces Drogo as an equal, shifting their marriage dynamic
An assassin enters Bran's room with a Valyrian steel dagger to kill him
Catelyn fights the assassin bare-handed
Bran's direwolf leaps in and tears out the assassin's throat
Catelyn resolves to ride south secretly to warn Ned about the Lannister threat

episodes.theme: the cost of power and the protection of innocence

interactions.description: all three plotlines explore how power dynamics affect the vulnerable—Bran, Lady, and Daenerys
the assassination attempt confirms Catelyn's suspicions about the Lannisters, driving her to act
the audience knows Jaime pushed Bran, making Ned's loyalty to Robert tragically misplaced

events.event: Jon arrives at Castle Black and discovers most recruits are criminals and outcasts
Jon dominates in the practice yard due to his superior training
Jon's dominance earns him resentment from other recruits
Tyrion advises Jon to stop acting like a privileged lord's son
Jon begins helping the weaker boys train
Ned arrives in King's Landing and takes his seat on the Small Council
Ned meets Littlefinger, Varys, and Grand Maester Pycelle
Ned learns the Crown owes millions to the Lannisters and Iron Bank
Robert demands a lavish tournament despite Ned's objections
Catelyn arrives in King's Landing in secret
Littlefinger arranges a private meeting between Catelyn and Ned
Catelyn shows Ned the Valyrian steel dagger from Bran's assassination attempt
Littlefinger identifies the dagger as one he lost to Tyrion in a bet
Ned and Catelyn agree she must return north while he investigates
Arya chases cats and stumbles into the Red Keep's cellar
Arya overhears two men discussing Stark-Lannister tension and war plans
Arya fails to convince Ned of the conversation's significance
Daenerys commands the khalasar to halt so she can explore on foot
Viserys strikes Daenerys for defying his authority
Dothraki guards intervene and Daenerys allows them to humiliate Viserys
Viserys realizes his sister is no longer under his control

episodes.theme: Power comes from respect, not birthright

interactions.description: Both Jon and Daenerys learn that true leadership requires earning respect rather than relying on privilege
Catelyn's arrival in King's Landing merges her protective mission with Ned's investigation
Both plotlines reveal how financial irresponsibility and political machinations threaten the realm's stability

events.event: Ned visits the blacksmith's apprentice Gendry, who bears a striking resemblance to young Robert Baratheon
Ned realizes Robert has bastard children throughout King's Landing, all with black hair
Ned obtains the genealogical history book Jon Arryn was studying before his death
Tyrion departs Castle Black heading south
Tyrion stops at Winterfell and gives Bran designs for a special saddle
Robb receives Tyrion coldly, suspicious of all Lannisters
Daenerys discovers she is pregnant with Drogo's child
Jorah tells Daenerys privately that a Targaryen heir changes the political calculation in Westeros
Daenerys announces her pregnancy to Drogo, who declares the child will be the Stallion Who Mounts the World
Viserys grows increasingly bitter that the Dothraki show him no deference
The tournament of the Hand begins at King's Landing
Ser Gregor Clegane kills Ser Hugh of the Vale during a joust
Littlefinger tells Sansa the story of Gregor and the Hound
Ned suspects Ser Hugh's death was not accidental and that someone is eliminating witnesses
Catelyn recognizes Tyrion among travelers at the Crossroads Inn
Catelyn calls on her father's bannermen and arrests Tyrion for attempting to murder Bran
Catelyn takes Tyrion east toward the Eyrie to avoid Lannister pursuit

episodes.theme: the dangerous pursuit of truth and justice in a world where knowledge is deadly

interactions.description: all three plotlines involve characters discovering dangerous truths that change their position and put them at risk
Catelyn's arrest of Tyrion directly merges their conflicting goals
all involve the revelation of heirs and bloodlines that threaten existing power structures

events.event: Robert receives word that Daenerys is pregnant and orders her assassination
Ned argues against murdering Daenerys and her unborn child, calling it dishonorable
Robert refuses to relent on the assassination order
Ned resigns as Hand of the King and announces he will return to Winterfell
Varys warns Ned that his investigation has made him a target and Jon Arryn was poisoned
Catelyn's party is ambushed by hill tribes on the road to the Eyrie
Tyrion fights alongside his captors to survive the ambush
They reach the Eyrie where Lysa imprisons Tyrion in the sky cells
Tyrion discovers Lysa has already convicted him in her mind
Jon and fellow recruits including Samwell prepare to take their vows at Castle Black
Jon is assigned to stewards rather than rangers, which he considers an insult
Sam explains that the Lord Commander's steward is being groomed for command
Arya practices sword training with Syrio Forel in King's Landing
Ned watches the lesson and his expression darkens, reminded that war is coming
Littlefinger takes Ned to a brothel where another of Robert's bastards lives
Ned realizes Joffrey, Myrcella, and Tommen are products of Cersei and Jaime's incest
Jaime confronts Ned in the streets with armed men over Tyrion's arrest
Jaime's soldiers kill Ned's guards and Jaime wounds Ned before riding away

episodes.theme: the cost of honor in a world that rewards pragmatism over principle

interactions.description: all three plotlines explore characters choosing between honor and survival—Ned resigning over principle, Tyrion fighting pragmatically, Jon accepting his assignment
Tyrion's arrest by Catelyn directly leads to Jaime attacking Ned, merging the plotlines through violence
the audience knows Ned has discovered the truth about the royal children's parentage, but Robert and others remain unaware

events.event: Ned wakes from his injuries to find Robert has returned from hunting
Robert demands Ned resume his duties as Hand, threatening to give the position to Jaime if he refuses
Ned reluctantly agrees to continue as Hand
Robert rides out hunting again, leaving Ned to sit the Iron Throne
Villagers petition the court about Ser Gregor Clegane's raids in the Riverlands
Ned recognizes this as Lannister provocation to draw the Tullys into conflict
Ned sends Lord Beric Dondarrion to bring Gregor to justice and summons Tywin Lannister
Tyrion demands a trial by combat rather than face Lysa's biased judgment
Tyrion recruits the sellsword Bronn to fight as his champion
Bronn defeats Ser Vardis Egen using speed and cunning
Lysa is forced to release Tyrion, who walks out free with Bronn
Viserys arrives drunk at a Dothraki feast and draws his sword in Vaes Dothrak
Viserys threatens Daenerys and demands the crown Drogo promised him
Drogo melts gold and pours it over Viserys's head, killing him
Daenerys watches her brother die without flinching and declares he was no dragon
Jon Snow and Sam take their vows before a heart tree beyond the Wall
Ghost emerges carrying a severed human hand, alerting the Watch that the dead are stirring
Ned studies the genealogy book and confirms all Baratheons have black-haired children
Ned holds the secret that could destroy the Lannisters but wrestles with how to use it

episodes.theme: the price of truth and justice in a world where power trumps honor

interactions.description: all three plotlines involve characters confronting harsh truths and making decisive choices that transform them
Ned's discovery about the royal children directly threatens the succession and his role as Hand
both Tyrion and Daenerys face life-or-death moments that require them to embrace their true nature

events.event: Ned confronts Cersei in the godswood, revealing he knows her children are Jaime's
Ned offers Cersei the chance to flee with her children before Robert returns
Cersei tells Ned that in the game of thrones, you win or you die
Robert is brought back mortally wounded by a boar during the hunt
Robert dictates his will to Ned, naming him Protector of the Realm
Ned writes 'rightful heir' instead of Joffrey's name in the will
Robert dies
Ned sends a letter to Stannis informing him of the truth about Cersei's children
Ned asks Littlefinger to secure the City Watch's loyalty
Littlefinger advises Ned to make peace with the Lannisters and rule through Joffrey
Ned refuses to compromise with what he sees as a usurper
Renly offers Ned backing of his household guard to seize Joffrey
Ned refuses Renly's offer, unwilling to frighten a child with soldiers
Renly flees King's Landing
A wine merchant tries to poison Daenerys
Jorah Mormont intervenes and exposes the assassination plot
The assassination attempt enrages Drogo, who vows to conquer Westeros
Bodies of two dead rangers are brought inside Castle Black
The corpses reanimate as wights in the night
Jon fights a wight that attacks the Lord Commander
Jon saves the Lord Commander by setting the wight on fire

episodes.theme: Honor versus pragmatism in the face of mortal danger

interactions.description: All three plotlines involve characters facing deadly threats that force them to choose between honor and survival
Ned's investigation directly triggers the succession crisis when he confronts Cersei and Robert dies
Both Ned and Jon face supernatural/political threats that test their commitment to duty over self-preservation

events.event: Ned enters the throne room to present Robert's will naming him Protector of the Realm
Cersei tears up the will and declares Joffrey king
On Littlefinger's signal, the gold cloaks turn on Ned's men, killing his household guard
Littlefinger puts a dagger to Ned's throat and betrays him
Ned is arrested and imprisoned in the dungeons beneath the Red Keep
Sansa is kept in her chambers under Lannister guard
Syrio Forel fights Lannister soldiers with a wooden practice sword to help Arya escape
Syrio is overwhelmed and presumably killed
Arya recovers Needle and hides in the streets of King's Landing
Drogo's khalasar raids a village to fund the invasion of Westeros
Daenerys orders the Dothraki to stop raping the captive women and takes them under her protection
Mago challenges Daenerys's order, angering Drogo's warriors
Drogo fights and kills Mago in single combat despite taking a chest wound
Mirri Maz Duur offers to treat Drogo's wound and Daenerys accepts her help
Lord Commander Mormont rewards Jon with Longclaw for saving his life from the wight
Jon receives word that Ned has been arrested for treason and attempts to desert
Sam, Grenn, and Pyp convince Jon to honor his vow and return
Tyrion recruits the hill tribes by promising Tywin's gold and weapons
Tyrion arrives at Tywin's war camp where an army is assembling against the Riverlands

episodes.theme: the cost of honor in a world without it

interactions.description: all three heroes face moments where their principles are tested against survival
Ned's downfall directly triggers the succession crisis and puts his daughters in danger
both Tyrion and Arya must use cunning and allies to escape deadly situations
Daenerys gains power through moral authority while Ned loses it through moral inflexibility

events.event: Robb calls the northern bannermen to war and marches south with twenty thousand men
Catelyn negotiates passage at the Twins by promising Robb will marry a Frey daughter and Arya will marry a Frey son
Robb splits his forces, sending a feint toward Tywin while leading the bulk to Riverrun
The feint engages Tywin's forces in the Battle of the Green Fork
Tyrion fights alongside the hill tribes and is knocked unconscious early in the fighting
Tywin wins the battle but realizes too late that the main Stark army was not there
Robb's true force ambushes Jaime's army at the Battle of the Whispering Wood
The Starks win decisively and Robb personally captures Jaime Lannister
Catelyn confronts the captive Jaime, who taunts her but cannot deny his vulnerability
Varys visits Ned in the dungeon and urges him to confess to treason for Sansa's sake
Ned agrees to lie for his daughter's sake
Ned is brought before the crowd and publicly confesses that he conspired against the rightful king
Joffrey orders Ned's execution despite expectations of mercy
Ser Ilyn Payne beheads Ned Stark with his own sword as Sansa screams
Arya is grabbed by Yoren who shields her eyes and pulls her away
Drogo's wound festers into a deadly infection
Daenerys desperately asks Mirri Maz Duur to use blood magic to save Drogo
Mirri agrees but warns that only death pays for life
Daenerys goes into premature labor and is carried into the tent where dark magic is being performed

episodes.theme: the price of power and the consequences of choosing honor over survival

interactions.description: all three plotlines explore sacrifice—Ned sacrifices his honor for family, Daenerys risks everything for love, Robb commits to war for justice
Catelyn's negotiations enable Robb's military strategy, their goals align in rescuing Ned
the audience knows Ned's confession is false while the crowd believes it, and Joffrey's cruelty surprises even his allies

events.event: Daenerys wakes to learn Mirri Maz Duur's blood magic saved Drogo's life but destroyed his mind
Daenerys discovers her son was stillborn and monstrous, the death that paid for Drogo's life
Mirri reveals she acted deliberately as revenge for the destruction of her village
Most of the khalasar abandons Daenerys for a new khal, leaving her with a small band of loyal followers
Jorah urges Daenerys to flee east, but she chooses a different path
Joffrey forces Sansa to look at her father's head mounted on a spike
Sansa nearly pushes Joffrey from the rampart but the Hound stops her
The Hound quietly wipes blood from Sansa's lip after Joffrey has a guard strike her
Arya travels north with Yoren disguised as a boy named Arry
Gendry, Robert's unrecognized bastard, travels with the Night's Watch recruits
Robb's bannermen debate their next move at the Twins
Robb proposes marching south to avenge Ned but cannot serve a Baratheon king
Greatjon Umber draws his sword and declares Robb the King in the North
The northern lords kneel and take up the chant, crowning Robb king
Catelyn visits captive Jaime and strikes him for what the Lannisters have done
Lord Commander Mormont tells Jon his duty lies with the Watch, not the war of kings
Mormont announces a great ranging to find Benjen and investigate the White Walkers
Jon rides out with the Night's Watch into the haunted forest
Daenerys builds a funeral pyre for Drogo and places her dragon eggs on it
Daenerys ties Mirri Maz Duur to the flames as a final sacrifice
Daenerys walks into the fire herself over Jorah's protests
Daenerys emerges unburned with three living dragons, the first in over a century
Her followers kneel before Daenerys as the Mother of Dragons

interactions.description: all three plotlines involve characters choosing their destiny through sacrifice and commitment to a greater purpose
both deal with the aftermath of Ned's death and its impact on the Stark family
Gendry's presence among the Night's Watch recruits connects Robert's bastards to Arya's escape
